title: Navro Market Debut
description: A clean launch story for a new market entrant, built around visibility, trust, and local relevance.
problem: Navro needed to launch in a new market with low brand awareness, product messaging that had not yet been localized, and limited trust-building touchpoints for its target audience.
solution: Connect Vietnam built a launch story around credibility, local relevance, and trial-driven engagement, giving the brand a clear communication route from awareness to consideration.
work: Developed the launch narrative and key messages
Built the PR kit, media angles, and Q&A materials
Guided the visual direction for digital campaign assets
Planned the launch-phase social content flow
Advised on partner and first-customer outreach
results: Created a unified message system for the launch campaign
Improved brand clarity across digital and media touchpoints
Reduced preparation time for sales and partner-facing communication materials

title: CBR Story Grid
description: A repeatable content system that turns expert knowledge into clear, consistent storylines.
problem: CBR had strong subject-matter knowledge, but its content was fragmented, difficult to maintain consistently, and not yet shaped into clear storylines for its audience.
solution: Connect Vietnam designed a repeatable content grid with clear pillars, enabling the team to publish consistently while keeping the brand voice and expert positioning aligned.
work: Audited existing content and identified message gaps
Built content pillars and a message house
Designed scalable social content formats
Wrote sample captions and headline routes by topic
Proposed a monthly content rhythm
results: Created a practical content system for the internal team
Improved consistency between expert messaging and social storytelling
Built a reusable format library for future campaigns

title: Culture Pop-up Lab
description: A modular event concept that turns audience insight into hands-on brand participation.
problem: The client wanted a live engagement format, but needed a concept flexible enough for multiple venues and compelling enough to invite natural audience participation.
solution: Connect Vietnam translated cultural and behavioral insights into a modular pop-up concept where audiences had reasons to touch, try, participate, and share.
work: Researched audience insights and the participation journey
Built the activation concept and experience flow
Proposed layout, content touchpoints, and check-in mechanics
Developed onsite guidance for the activation team
Designed a post-event engagement measurement framework
results: Created a flexible concept that could travel across venues
Increased the quality of interaction between audience and brand
Delivered assets and operating scripts for future activations

title: Small dots. Big movement.
description: Campaign platforms shaped for fast rollout and long-term recall.
problem: The brand needed a big idea that was simple to remember, broad enough for multi-channel rollout, and flexible enough to support smaller initiatives over time.
solution: Connect Vietnam developed a campaign platform based on the idea of turning small touchpoints into larger momentum, allowing the concept to scale across social, PR, and activation.
work: Explored brand truth and audience tension
Developed campaign territories and tagline routes
Created the concept-level key visual direction
Proposed channel-specific activation ideas
Prepared a concise campaign playbook for rollout teams
results: Created a memorable and expandable campaign foundation
Aligned content, design, and media teams around one creative route
Shortened the concept approval cycle for follow-up assets

title: Signals & Conversations
description: A webinar-led content route connecting expert voices, press angles, and post-event social proof.
problem: The client wanted to use webinars to build expert credibility, but needed stronger content structure before, during, and after the event to sustain media and community conversations.
solution: Connect Vietnam created a webinar-led content route connecting expert voices, media angles, and post-event social proof, turning a single session into a sequence of communication assets.
work: Designed the webinar topic and discussion structure
Built the speaker brief, run-of-show, and prompting questions
Developed pre-event and post-event media angles
Edited recap content, quote cards, and social cutdowns
Proposed a follow-up flow for interested audiences
results: Turned event content into multiple post-event communication formats
Improved the usability of expert quotes for PR and social channels
Created ongoing dialogue instead of a one-off webinar moment
